items.title: Training and inference
items.description: fit() runs only on D_train with labels; validation and test rows are scored via predict() with frozen θ — the same train-then-infer pattern that defines the supervised ML lifecycle.

items.title: Cross-validation
items.description: When data is scarce or a single validation slice is too noisy, k-fold cross-validation on the training portion estimates generalization more stably — often paired with a small locked test set.

items.title: Generalization
items.description: Validation and test errors quantify generalization to unseen data — this topic supplies the partitions; Generalization defines what those held-out metrics mean.

items.title: Data leakage
items.description: Leakage — preprocessing on all data, duplicate entities across splits, or tuning on the test set — makes validation and test rows no longer truly unseen, inflating reported scores.

items.title: Early stopping
items.description: Training monitors validation loss each epoch and stops when it stops improving — validation is the feedback channel that prevents overfitting during iterative training.

items.title: Machine learning
items.description: Every supervised ML pipeline separates learning data from evaluation data — train/val/test splits are the foundational hold-out pattern before deployment.
